Bulbasaur.strategy_guide: Bulbasaur is a Grass/Poison boss with very low attack at Tier 1. Bring Charizard (Fire Spin / Blast Burn) or a Flying-type. Spam fast attacks to fill the Max Meter quickly. No dodging is needed — use all 3 Max Attack turns for easy KO.

Charmander.strategy_guide: Charmander is a pure Fire boss. Bring Squirtle or Blastoise with Water Gun and Hydro Cannon. The Max Geyser will chunk a huge portion of HP. No need to dodge — cycle fast moves and secure the Max Phase.

Squirtle.strategy_guide: Squirtle is a pure Water boss. Lead with Bulbasaur or Venusaur. Vine Whip charges the meter very quickly. Execute Max Overgrowth as soon as the Max Phase triggers for massive damage.

Wooloo.strategy_guide: Wooloo is a Normal-type boss. Bring Machamp or Falinks with Counter. Fighting-type Max Knuckle will deal super-effective damage. This is one of the easiest Tier 1 battles — no strategy needed.

Skwovet.strategy_guide: Skwovet is a Normal-type boss identical in strategy to Wooloo. Use Counter-based Fighting-types for quick meter fill and easy Max Phase KO.

Beldum.strategy_guide: Beldum has high Defense. Use Charizard (Fire Spin / Blast Burn) for Max Flare or Gengar for Ghost-type pressure. When the boss telegraphs a targeted Iron Head, swap to your tank Pokémon to absorb it and preserve your attacker's HP.

Falinks.strategy_guide: Falinks is a pure Fighting boss. Bring Charizard or Gengar — Flying and Psychic moves are super-effective. Swap to a bulkier Pokémon when targeted to avoid major HP loss on your primary attacker.

Gastly.strategy_guide: Gastly is a Ghost/Poison boss. Bring Metagross (Zen Headbutt / Meteor Mash) or Gengar for mirrored Ghost attacks. Proactive tank-swapping is effective here — Gastly hits moderately hard at Tier 3.

Drilbur.strategy_guide: Drilbur is a Ground-type boss. Bring Venusaur or Blastoise with Grass/Water moves. Both types deal super-effective damage. Swap to a tank before targeted Mud Bomb attacks to protect your attacker.

Registeel.strategy_guide: Dynamax Registeel is extremely tough with massive Defense and solid attack power. Lead with Charizard (Fire type has 2x effectiveness). Swap to your tank when Iron Head or Hyper Beam is telegraphed. Time your Max Flare or G-Max Wildfire carefully — at Tier 5 you need all 3 Max turns dealing damage. Use Max Spirit only if critical health. Defeat before 6 minutes or enrage will overwhelm you.

Venusaur.strategy_guide: Gigantamax Venusaur has 90,000 HP and hits very hard. This is a large-group battle. Lead with G-Max Charizard or strong Fire-types. Proactive tank swapping is mandatory — its targeted Solar Beam can wipe unprepared Pokémon. Collect ALL Max Energy bubbles. Use Max Spirit when party health drops below 40%. Aim to enter Max Phase multiple times.

Charizard.strategy_guide: Gigantamax Charizard hits extremely hard with Fire-type attacks. Lead with G-Max Blastoise or Venusaur. Its targeted Blast Burn/Overheat is brutal — skilled players dodge (swipe) while others use a tank-swap. After 6 minutes it enrages with 6x attack power, making survival nearly impossible. Pressure it hard and use G-Max Cannonade for maximum water damage.

Blastoise.strategy_guide: Gigantamax Blastoise has very high Defense. Lead with G-Max Venusaur — Vine Whip is one of the fastest energy generators in the game. Spam fast moves to enter Max Phase ASAP. Targeted Hydro Pump hits very hard at this tier. Swap to your tank to absorb it whenever telegraphed.

Gengar.strategy_guide: Gigantamax Gengar hits incredibly hard. Lead with Metagross (Zen Headbutt / Meteor Mash — Psychic resists Ghost and deals super-effective damage) or Gengar for mirrored pressure. Tank-swap is critical before Shadow Ball or Sludge Bomb targeted hits. G-Max Terror has reduced PP-like constraints in GO, making Gengar a threatening boss that demands a real tank in slot 3.

Toxtricity.strategy_guide: Gigantamax Toxtricity is an Electric/Poison boss. Ground-type moves deal 2.56x damage. Lead with Excadrill (Mud-Slap / Earthquake) — this is one of the cleanest type-advantage matchups in Max Battles. Max Quake absolutely demolishes it. Dodge or tank-swap Wild Charge targeted attacks. G-Max Stun Shock does no additional effect in GO beyond damage.
